respondent_decision_he: החלטה מנהלית של שר התחבורה הנוגעת לרישוי בתחום התחבורה, אשר העותר טען כי איננה סבירה
respondent_decision_en: Administrative decision by the Minister of Transportation in a transport-licensing matter, which the petitioner challenged as unreasonable
summary_he: בית המשפט העליון, בשבתו כבג"ץ ובהרכב של שלושה שופטים, דחה את העתירה פה אחד. בחוות-הדעת המובילה, שכתב השופט אהרן ברק, נוסחה לראשונה עילת אי-הסבירות כעילה עצמאית לביקורת שיפוטית על החלטות מנהליות, ולא רק כאינדיקציה לפגמים אחרים. פורסם בפ"ד ל(2) 94.
summary_en: The Supreme Court, sitting as the High Court of Justice in a three-justice panel, unanimously dismissed the petition. In the lead opinion, authored by Justice Aharon Barak, the reasonableness ground was articulated for the first time as an independent ground of judicial review over administrative decisions, rather than merely as an indicator of other defects. Reported at P"D 30(2) 94.
notes: Pre-digital era ruling — no supremedecisions.court.gov.il URL exists for this 1976 judgment. The official_url field points to the Cardozo Versa topic page on judicial reasonableness, which is the closest authoritative English-language secondary source; no full Versa translation of Dakka itself appears to exist. Canonical Hebrew citation: פ"ד ל(2) 94. Methodology trade-offs: (1) Schema 'outcome' enum has no 'declarative' value; 'dismissed' is used because the petition itself was denied, with the doctrinal significance arising from Justice Barak's articulation of reasonableness as an independent ground in the reasoning. (2) Exact ruling-date day/month not verified from the pre-digital record; 1976-01-01 is a placeholder year-only date drawn from the user-provided context (P"D 30(2), 1976). (3) Two additional panel members are recorded as 'unverified' placeholders; only Justice Barak (lead-opinion author) is documented from the user-provided context. The other two seats on the standard 3-justice HCJ panel of the period must be filled in from the printed P"D before publication. Flag for source-link verification before any external citation.
